# Spec context: §7.4 — Voice overlay

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#voice_overlay>
- Cached at: `crates/kaijutsu-abc/docs/abc-spec-cache.md` (line 1994)
- Section: 7. Multiple voices
- Subsection: 7.4 Voice overlay

Reproduced verbatim from the ABC v2.1 standard by Chris Walshaw,

## Preceding prose

> *VOLATILE:* See [above](#multiple_voices "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> The `&` operator may be used to temporarily overlay several voices within one measure. Each `&` operator sets the time point of the music back by one bar line, and the notes which follow it form a temporary voice in parallel with the preceding one. This may only be used to add one complete bar's wor
> Example:
> [![](/wiki/_media/abc:standard:overlay1-80.png)](/wiki/_detail/abc:standard:overlay1-80.png?id=abc%3Astandard%3Av2.1 "abc:standard:overlay1-80.png")
> Words in `w:` lines (and symbols in `s:` lines) are matched to the corresponding notes as per the normal rules for lyric alignment (see [lyrics](#lyrics "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵")), disregarding any overlay in the accompanying [music code](#music_code_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> *Example:*
